preview_text:
The model favors Senegal at 61%, with Iraq given a 13% chance to pull off an upset. The market consensus—both sportsbook and Polymarket—is notably more confident in a Senegal victory, rating it near 71%, leaving Iraq with roughly 10%.

The model's spread suggests a moderately competitive match. Senegal's advantage is clear but not dominant; a draw at 27% is plausible and materially more likely than an Iraq win. The market tightens the outcome considerably, compressing the draw probability to around 19% and offering less upside to the underdog, implying higher confidence that Senegal will force a decisive result.

The two sources diverge meaningfully on Senegal's win probability, with the market roughly 10 percentage points higher than the model. That gap widens the daylight between a draw and an Iraq victory in the market's framing. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is more accurate; the discrepancy may reflect different treatment of available information or underlying match dynamics not captured in the model inputs.
